hypothesis: Incorporating explicit electronegativity difference tensors between shell 1 and shell 2 elements as an additional conditioning signal in the latent diffusion model will improve adsorption energy prediction accuracy and generation fidelity for multi-adsorbate systems, because charge transfer effects at the active site—which are governed by inter-shell electronegativity gradients—are currently unrepresented in CompGen's conditioning scheme yet are known to modulate binding energetics independently of ensemble and ligand composition counts.
